lines.en_lit: Rabbi Yonatan says: from this by itself, and from that by itself Rabbi Yoshiya said to him: But wasn’t it already stated: once

Rabbi Yonatan said to him: But wasn’t it already stated: “Of the blood of the bull and of the blood of the goat” If so, why is “once” stated? to tell you once and not two from the blood of the bull; once and not two from the blood of the goat.

It was taught another “And he shall take of the blood of the bull and of the blood of the goat” that should be mixed together. you say that they should be mixed together, or perhaps that is not from this by itself and from that by itself? the verse states: “Once.” And unattributed is in accordance with Rabbi Yoshiya,

he placed from the full into the empty Rami bar Ḥama raised a dilemma before Rav Ḥisda: What is he placed bowl inside bowl and collected the blood in type with its type interpose, Or does not interpose
said to him: We learned He placed from the full into the empty What, is it not he inserted full bowl into empty bowl?
No, he poured full bowl into empty bowl. as taught first He poured the blood of the bull into the blood of the goat. in order to mix them very well.
Come hear was standing on top of a vessel, or on the foot of another is disqualified, A foot is different, as he cannot nullify it.

Some say this is dilemma he raised before Is manner of service; or is this not manner of service, Come hear which the school of Rabbi Yishmael taught: all the service vessels, with which they serve in the Sanctuary” two vessels and one service,

Rami bar Ḥama raised dilemma before Rav Ḥisda What is he placed bast, inside the bowl, and collected the blood in type with not its type interpose; or it does not interpose? since the blood seeps does not interpose? Or perhaps no different,
said to him We learned He sprinkles and continues until he reaches the sponge. Water is different, as it is thin
Some say this is how resolved for of the blood it is valid, of the handful it is disqualified.
